claims: All 4 PRD promise contracts map to existing public boundaries
Each promise has at least one non-vacuous public-boundary test
P3 report-only invariant enforced at activation layer, not only at draft creation
Out-of-scope/anti-goals in PRD are consistent with source (no policy mutation, gate authority unchanged)

criteria: Public boundaries named in PRD exist in source (verified)
Each P1-P4 promise has a mapped non-vacuous test (verified by read)
P3 immutable->report_only enforced and forbidden-activation tested (verified)
Tests observed passing via pytest (NOT done -> unknown)
confidence.rationale: PRD promises, boundaries, the P3 enforcement guard, migration version, and test non-vacuity were all confirmed by direct source reads. Confidence is held below 0.95 because pytest was not executed this session (Bash denied), so test GREEN status is unobserved, and prd.md sha256 was not recomputed (content read directly instead).

payload.content: PRD 'AutoResearch Signal Generator And Gated Runner' reviewed against current source. All four promise contracts (P1-P4) map to real public boundaries with non-vacuous public-boundary tests over a real State DB. P3 immutability is enforced at the activation layer (state.py:1024 promotes only draft rows) and tested for the forbidden outcome. Migration v9 matches the PRD claim. Anti-goals (no policy mutation, gate authority unchanged, default_change_allowed=false) are honored in every event payload. Accept; sole residual is pytest not executed this session.

assumptions_to_verify: Named tests are actually GREEN under pytest (currently assumed from structure)
DEFAULT_IMMUTABLE_PATHS includes supervisor/state.py (P3 test relies on this; test asserts report_only so consistent)
Postgres activate_autoresearch_experiment mirrors SQLite draft-only promotion guard
contradictions_checked: PRD 'move to report_only when surface immutable' vs activation: state.py:1024 only promotes draft rows -> consistent, no contradiction
P1 'exactly one inert experiment' vs idempotency: upsert created-flag with 'if not created: continue' prevents duplicate rows/events -> consistent
Clusters with no implicated paths: _cluster_implicated_paths falls back to _default_paths_for_signal -> does not contradict PRD
missing_evidence: Executed pytest run for tests/test_autoresearch_generator.py with pass/fail output
Recomputed sha256 of prd.md matching handoff value 1a24a989...
Postgres-lane parity execution confirming the Alembic migration 20260610_0003 applies live
critical_review.strongest_objection: This is a prd_review but the implementation already exists, and pytest was not run this session, so test GREEN status is unverified — acceptance rests on structural/non-vacuity reading of tests rather than an executed green receipt.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: An executed pytest run showing any of the six tests failing, evidence that report-only rows can be promoted to runnable, or a prd.md sha256 mismatch against the handoff packet.

Supervisor-owned workflow gate: issues_review.

Intent:
Draft operator-activated AutoResearch experiments automatically from recurring supervisor failure signals, executed via the durable evaluator lane.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

claims: issues.md is immutable_by_worker=false in handoff (not rewritten by this review)
Generator implements all 3 PRD public boundaries: generate_autoresearch_experiment_drafts, activate_autoresearch_experiment, run_runnable_autoresearch_experiments
Bulk of ACs are both source and test backed; residual gaps are coverage items not decomposition defects

criteria: PRD promises enumerated and each mapped to a slice with no orphan: PASS
ACs traceable to real source symbols: PASS
ACs traceable to non-vacuous tests: PASS for majority; PARTIAL for S3-AC3 and S4-AC4
Artifact hashes re-verified: NOT DONE (bash denied)
confidence.rationale: Issues decomposition maps cleanly to PRD P1-P4 with no orphans; schema, state APIs, generator boundaries verified in current source; 9 tests read as non-vacuous with genuine negative/forbidden-outcome assertions. Lowered from higher because two ACs (S3-AC3 activation ledger evidence, S4-AC4 failed-run terminal) are source-enforced but lack dedicated tests, and pytest was not executed plus artifact hashes not re-verified (bash denied).

payload.content: issues.md decomposes PRD P1-P4 into 4 non-orphan slices (ledger queue, clustering/drafting, operator activation, gated runner). Every slice's primary behavior is source-implemented and test-backed with non-vacuous assertions (below-threshold->empty, duplicate->empty, draft-no-execute->job_count 0, weekly cap 1/empty, immutable->report_only stays report_only). Residual coverage gaps: S3-AC3 activation ledger event fields untested (source-enforced), S4-AC4 failed-run terminal path untested, S2-AC2 budget/timeout not asserted on row. Accept the gate; flag gaps to tdd.

assumptions_to_verify: That the 9 tests pass when executed (read-verified non-vacuous, not run)
That on-disk issues.md/prd.md sha256 equal handoff values 4c9b78e5.../1a24a989... (hash command denied)
That run_autoresearch_fixture live path used by the runner is the same durable evaluator lane referenced by the PRD (asserted via metric_source=='evaluator_execution' + workflow_job_count==1 in test)
contradictions_checked: Checked whether any slice maps to a PRD Out-Of-Scope item (policy overlays, proposal derivation, gate authority change) - none do
Checked whether activate could promote report_only rows - it cannot (state.py:1024 acts only when status=='draft')
Checked whether draft rows could execute - mark_run_started guards WHERE status='runnable' and runner only lists status='runnable' (generator.py:244), test confirms job_count 0 for draft
Checked migration ordering: v9 down_revision 20260610_0002, schema_migrations EXPECTED v9 appended after v8 - consistent
missing_evidence: A test asserting the autoresearch_experiment_activation_recorded event payload (operator, approval_channel, automatic_policy_mutation=False, gate_advanced=False)
A test exercising run_runnable_autoresearch_experiments failed branch -> status 'failed' terminal row + autoresearch_experiment_auto_run_failed event
pytest execution result for the focused suite
Independent shasum confirmation that on-disk artifacts match handoff sha256 (bash denied)
critical_review.strongest_objection: Two acceptance criteria assert ledger/terminal-status behavior that is only source-enforced, not test-asserted: S3-AC3 requires activation to emit operator-triggered ledger evidence with no policy mutation / no gate advance (generator.py:203-218) and S4-AC4 requires deterministic terminal status + ledger event for BOTH completed and failed runs, yet only the completed path is tested. An issues gate that accepts these ACs as 'test-backed' would be overstating coverage.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Discovering an uncovered PRD promise with no slice, a slice mapping to an Out-Of-Scope behavior, a state API that lets a report_only/draft row execute, or an AC whose source implementation contradicts the stated allowed/forbidden outcome would move this to revise/deny.

payload.block:
Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [reviewer_contract_unmet] unknown (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [reviewer_contract_unmet] unknown (source_run_id=aa628b4d-67fc-4210-8dd8-1ecb905e38ea): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=aa628b4d-67fc-4210-8dd8-1ecb905e38ea): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: tdd_review.

Intent:
Draft operator-activated AutoResearch experiments automatically from recurring supervisor failure signals, executed via the durable evaluator lane.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [reviewer_contract_unmet] unknown (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
2. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=bcf4a876-3308-4dd4-8c5b-b128b71d564a): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [reviewer_contract_unmet] unknown (source_run_id=aa628b4d-67fc-4210-8dd8-1ecb905e38ea): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=aa628b4d-67fc-4210-8dd8-1ecb905e38ea): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.

claims: 6 plan tests exist verbatim and map to PRD P1-P4 with no orphan tests
Tests are public-boundary and non-vacuous against a real State database
Forbidden outcomes are asserted for each PRD promise
Schema migration v9 and Postgres Alembic parity are covered in modified regression tests
pytest was not executed in this review (Bash approval denied); all verification done via Read/Grep on current source

criteria: Plan tests map 1:1 to real tests and PRD promises: met
Tests invoke real existing public boundaries and State APIs: met
Forbidden outcomes asserted per promise: met
Schema/Postgres parity covered non-vacuously: met (in regression files, not enumerated in tdd.md)
Live pytest pass/fail evidence: not met (Bash denied)
confidence.rationale: Structural fidelity, public-boundary non-vacuity, forbidden-outcome coverage, and schema parity were verified directly against current source via Read/Grep. Confidence is held below 0.9 because pytest was not executed (test_status unknown/self_reported) and tests are GREEN-not-RED since implementation already exists, so the plan's RED phase is asserted rather than demonstrated.

payload.content: TDD plan's 6 named tests exist verbatim in tests/test_autoresearch_generator.py, map to PRD P1-P4 with no orphans, exercise real public boundaries (generate/activate/run) against a real State DB, and cover forbidden outcomes (below-threshold no-row, duplicate no-row, draft no-self-run + workflow_job_count==0, immutable mutable_paths==[], weekly cap). Schema migration v9 + Postgres Alembic 20260610_0003 are covered non-vacuously in modified regression tests. Accept; residuals are GREEN-not-RED and pytest-not-run (self_reported).

assumptions_to_verify: The 7 tests actually pass under pytest in CI
run_autoresearch_fixture live path records exactly one dual_agent_workflow_jobs row as the test expects
DEFAULT_IMMUTABLE_PATHS includes supervisor/state.py so the immutable test classifies report_only as intended
contradictions_checked: PRD claims schema migration v9 + Postgres Alembic exist -> confirmed schema_migrations.py:335-339 and migrations/versions/20260610_0003

PRD claims runnable rows execute via durable evaluator lane (not parallel mechanism) -> generator.run_runnable_autoresearch_experiments calls run_autoresearch_fixture(execution_mode=live) and test asserts workflow_job_count==1
Plan claims duplicate generation creates no row -> test asserts duplicate==[] and queue len==1
Plan claims immutable surfaces become report_only and cannot be promoted -> test asserts activation stays report_only and runner returns []
missing_evidence: Executed pytest pass/fail output for the 7 tests (Bash approval denied)
Demonstrated RED state for any plan test prior to implementation (impl already present)
A dedicated test asserting the activation ledger event payload invariants for Slice 3 AC3
critical_review.strongest_objection: The tdd.md plan enumerates only the 6 generator tests and omits the SQLite migration v9 / Postgres Alembic parity tests that the PRD Testing Decisions and Slice 1 acceptance criteria explicitly require, creating a traceability gap between the plan and the required coverage.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Evidence that a plan test is vacuous (passes against a stub), that a named public boundary or State API does not exist, that the durable-lane assertion can be satisfied by direct subprocess evaluation, or a pytest run showing any of the 7 tests failing.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=40f4ecea-e8bd-4639-aec6-27d686743e8f): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
2. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=40f4ecea-e8bd-4639-aec6-27d686743e8f): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=40f4ecea-e8bd-4639-aec6-27d686743e8f): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=40f4ecea-e8bd-4639-aec6-27d686743e8f): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.
5. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Draft operator-activated AutoResearch experiments automatically from recurring supervisor failure signals, executed via the durable evaluator lane.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=40f4ecea-e8bd-4639-aec6-27d686743e8f): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
2. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=40f4ecea-e8bd-4639-aec6-27d686743e8f): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=40f4ecea-e8bd-4639-aec6-27d686743e8f): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=40f4ecea-e8bd-4639-aec6-27d686743e8f): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.
5. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.

payload.content:
Independently review the implementation_plan gate for this tri-agent workflow.
Accept only if the gate should advance after reading the artifacts and Claude outcome.
Critical review: before accepting, try to disprove the Claude outcome and gate evidence. Identify the strongest reason this should not advance, missing evidence, contradictions checked, assumptions to verify, and what would change your decision. Keep this concise and auditable; do not include private chain-of-thought.
Return the structured critical_review object in the typed outcome.

Intent:
Draft operator-activated AutoResearch experiments automatically from recurring supervisor failure signals, executed via the durable evaluator lane.

payload.content: Implementation plan is structurally sound (11 files, risks, P1-P4 traceability) and static worktree checks mostly match Claude. Independent pytest disproves advancement: P2 test test_autoresearch_draft_cannot_run_until_operator_marks_runnable fails because report metric_source stays pending and validation_status is rejected while runner marks queue completed. Anti-bypass call path exists but durable-evaluator outcome contract is unmet. Decision: REJECT.

Independent pytest disproves advancement: P2 test test_autoresearch_draft_cannot_run_until_operator_marks_runnable fails because report metric_source stays pending and validation_status is rejected while runner marks queue completed. Anti-bypass call path exists but durable-evaluator outcome contract is unmet. Decision: REJECT.

critical_review.strongest_objection: P2 explicitly forbids activation lacking operator and channel metadata, but activate_autoresearch_experiment does not require non-empty operator or approval_channel, and the SQLite/Postgres state methods promote any draft to runnable without checking those values. The existing TDD test only exercises valid non-empty metadata, so the strongest operator-boundary contract is not proven and appears violated.

critical_review.strongest_objection: P2 explicitly forbids activation lacking operator and channel metadata, but activate_autoresearch_experiment does not require non-empty operator or approval_channel, and the SQLite/Postgres state methods promote any draft to runnable without checking those values. The existing TDD test only exercises valid non-empty metadata, so the strongest operator-boundary contract is not proven and appears violated.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: Add _require_operator-style validation before any draft can become runnable, add tests for empty operator and empty approval_channel rejection in both intended state lanes or public boundary behavior, and provide a green pytest receipt for the listed tests.

Anti-bypass call path exists but durable-evaluator outcome contract is unmet. Decision: REJECT.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: implementation_plan.

Intent:
Draft operator-activated AutoResearch experiments automatically from recurring supervisor failure signals, executed via the durable evaluator lane.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Corrective context from the previous round:
cursor_review_failed: P2 test fails: metric_source=pending, validation_status=rejected, runner still marks completed despite grill Finding 3 requiring accepted report + durable evaluator proof

claims: The implementation_plan should REVISE, not advance.
The defect is localized to runner completion-gating plus a missing plan requirement; the broader architecture is sound.
A live evaluator that genuinely executes and is accepted would pass P2, but the runner must still gate on acceptance to be correct.

criteria: Runner completion logic lacks acceptance check — VERIFIED generator.py:274-280
Orchestrator swallows evaluator errors and returns report — VERIFIED orchestrator.py:147-162,:212
Validation rejects pending metric_source — VERIFIED validation.py:100-106
evidence: Add a runner acceptance gate before complete_autoresearch_experiment_run(status='completed'): every report record must have metric_source=='evaluator_execution' and validation_status=='accepted' (recommendation != 'review_required'); otherwise terminate as 'failed'/non-completed.
Update implementation-plan Traceability/Risks to explicitly require the acceptance gate so P2 is satisfied and grill Finding 3 (accepted report + durable evaluator proof) is enforced.
Do not advance the gate until the P2 deterministic probe (test_autoresearch_draft_cannot_run_until_operator_marks_runnable) is green with a cited receipt.
confidence.rationale: Source trace across generator.py, orchestrator.py, validation.py, report.py deterministically confirms the corrective-context defect: a non-executed evaluator returns a report that the runner marks 'completed'. The P2 assertions cannot be met by the current runner. Below 0.9 because I could not personally run pytest (approval-blocked), so the RED status is self_reported via corrective context plus static corroboration.

payload.content: REVISE: the runner run_runnable_autoresearch_experiments (generator.py:274-280) marks experiments 'completed' unconditionally after run_autoresearch_fixture returns, without checking report record metric_source/validation_status. orchestrator.py:147-162 swallows EvaluatorContractError/TimeoutError and returns a normal report whose attempt stays metric_source='pending'; validation.py:100-106 then yields validation_status='rejected'. So a non-executed/rejected evaluator run is recorded 'completed', violating grill Finding 3 (accepted report + durable evaluator proof) and failing P2 (test_autoresearch_generator.py:208-210, which asserts metric_source=='evaluator_execution' and validation_status=='accepted'). Plan must add a runner acceptance gate and name it in Traceability/Risks.

assumptions_to_verify: That the live evaluator, when properly provisioned, returns metric_source='evaluator_execution' (otherwise P2 cannot pass even after the gate fix).
That complete_autoresearch_experiment_run supports a non-'completed' terminal status the runner can use when acceptance fails.
contradictions_checked: Stale MEMORY.md entry recorded a prior ACCEPT for this implplan; current corrective context + source trace contradict it, so memory was discounted per staleness guidance.
Plan Risks mention a 'naive runner could bypass the durable evaluator lane' but do NOT capture the actual defect (completed-without-acceptance); the plan understates the risk.
Grill Finding 3 is marked 'resolved' in the doc, but the runner source does not enforce its acceptance requirement.
missing_evidence: A green pytest receipt for the P2 test (pytest was approval-blocked).

Live durable-evaluator run logs showing whether the replay_corpus evaluator can actually reach metric_source='evaluator_execution'/accepted for the auto-generated experiment, to distinguish runner-gate bug from evaluator-environment failure.
A dual_agent_workflow_jobs row receipt from an accepted run (Finding 3 acceptance criterion).

critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A re-run showing the P2 test green with a cited receipt AND runner source that gates completion on every record being metric_source=='evaluator_execution'/validation_status=='accepted' (terminating non-accepted runs as failed), satisfying grill Finding 3.

Do not advance the gate until the P2 deterministic probe (test_autoresearch_draft_cannot_run_until_operator_marks_runnable) is green with a cited receipt.
objections: generator.py:274-280 stamps 'completed' with no metric_source/validation_status check; only an exception reaches the 'failed' path (:297-317).

orchestrator.py:147-162 catches EvaluatorContractError/TimeoutError, marks attempt failed but does NOT re-raise, then returns a normal report (:188,:212) — defeating the runner's exception-only failure path.

validation.py:100-106 rejects any attempt whose metric_source!='evaluator_execution', so a swallowed evaluator failure deterministically produces validation_status='rejected' yet the queue row is 'completed'.
No green pytest receipt obtained (pytest approval-blocked); P2 is RED per corrective context.

specialists.name: lead-source-tracer
specialists.objection: Runner marks 'completed' without verifying report acceptance (generator.py:274-280); orchestrator returns report after swallowing evaluator errors (orchestrator.py:147-162).

outcome.summary: REVISE: the runner run_runnable_autoresearch_experiments (generator.py:274-280) marks experiments 'completed' unconditionally after run_autoresearch_fixture returns, without checking report record metric_source/validation_status. orchestrator.py:147-162 swallows EvaluatorContractError/TimeoutError and returns a normal report whose attempt stays metric_source='pending'; validation.py:100-106 then yields validation_status='rejected'. So a non-executed/rejected evaluator run is recorded 'completed', violating grill Finding 3 (accepted report + durable evaluator proof) and failing P2 (test_autoresearch_generator.py:208-210, which asserts metric_source=='evaluator_execution' and validation_status=='accepted'). Plan must add a runner acceptance gate and name it in Traceability/Risks.

claims: The implementation_plan should REVISE, not advance.
The defect is localized to runner completion-gating plus a missing plan requirement; the broader architecture is sound.
A live evaluator that genuinely executes and is accepted would pass P2, but the runner must still gate on acceptance to be correct.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.
2. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=cocreate-global-checkpoint-purge-20260610): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: execution.

Intent:
Draft operator-activated AutoResearch experiments automatically from recurring supervisor failure signals, executed via the durable evaluator lane.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.
2. [timeout] worker_invocation (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
3. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.
4. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=vela2-slack-write-002): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
5. [blocked_without_probe_reason] resource_contention (source_run_id=cocreate-global-checkpoint-purge-20260610): Resolve the failing deterministic probe and cite its new green receipt.

claims: Implementation is complete across all 11 planned files and statically consistent with all 7 planned tests.
Durable evaluator lane is invoked live (run_autoresearch_fixture), not via direct subprocess — no bypass.
Idempotency enforced by signal_key UNIQUE + INSERT OR IGNORE rowcount; duplicate generation returns no new row.
Immutable-surface signals become report_only and are never promoted to runnable nor executed.
Weekly cap is enforced from persisted last_run_started_at, deterministic under supplied now.
Tests were NOT executed in this environment (pytest denied); GREEN status is static-only/self_reported.

evidence: ACCEPT: execution gate advances. Implementation complete and statically consistent with all 7 planned tests across 11 files; live durable-evaluator lane wired (anti-bypass); authority invariants (default_change_allowed=False, gate_authority=unchanged) enforced on every payload.
confidence.rationale: Implementation is complete and every one of the 7 planned tests maps to real, non-vacuous source symbols with correct semantics (idempotency, immutable classification, live-runner acceptance gate, weekly cap, dual-lane parity). Confidence is held below 0.9 solely because pytest could not be executed in this environment, so the RED->GREEN transition and runtime behavior are verified by static reading and the existing diff rather than an actual test run.

payload.content: AutoResearch hypothesis-generation implemented across 11 files (+640/-5 tracked + generator.py, test_autoresearch_generator.py, Alembic 0003). Generator clusters recurring failure/reviewer/probe/lesson signals, drafts idempotently by signal_key, classifies immutable surfaces as report_only, activates operator-approved drafts, and runs runnable experiments through the LIVE durable evaluator (run_autoresearch_fixture, not subprocess) with a weekly cap and an acceptance gate (validation_status==accepted AND metric_source==evaluator_execution). All 7 planned tests map to real non-vacuous symbols; dual-lane SQLite v9 + Postgres Alembic 0003 parity present. pytest is environment-denied so GREEN is static-only/self_reported.

assumptions_to_verify: pytest would pass if the approval to run it were granted.
run_autoresearch_fixture kwargs (output_dir, k_trials, etc.) align with the live orchestrator signature.
Alembic 0003 applies cleanly against a real Postgres instance.
contradictions_checked: FM-1.3 step-repetition: this turn adds NEW evidence (git diff --stat + targeted grep of generator.py internals) rather than re-submitting the prior handoff.
Anti-bypass lesson: confirmed run_autoresearch_fixture is the live durable path (import :12, call :264), not a re-introduced subprocess.
Authority invariants: confirmed no payload sets default_change_allowed True or mutates gate_authority.
Immutable classification: validation.py lists state.py (test 6 report_only) but not orchestrator.py (test 2 runnable) — no contradiction with both tests.
missing_evidence: Actual pytest run output for tests/test_autoresearch_generator.py (7 tests pass/fail).
Live Postgres execution of Alembic 0003 (parity asserted structurally only).
Confirmation that run_autoresearch_fixture's real signature matches generator.py:264 call kwargs at runtime.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The 7 planned tests were never actually executed — pytest and even plain python imports are environment-denied. The accept therefore rests on static source reading and the pre-existing diff, not on an observed RED->GREEN transition. A subtle runtime defect (fixture wiring, import cycle, SQL typo) could pass static review yet fail at runtime.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: An actual pytest run showing any of the 7 tests failing, or discovery that run_autoresearch_fixture's real signature/return shape differs from what generator.py:264 expects, would move this from accept to revise.

payload.content:
Supervisor-owned workflow gate: outcome_review.

Intent:
Draft operator-activated AutoResearch experiments automatically from recurring supervisor failure signals, executed via the durable evaluator lane.

Review this gate against the current source artifacts and return a typed dual_agent_outcome.
Use decisions/objections to say whether the gate should accept, revise, or deny.

Known failure modes to verify before claiming
These are advisory lessons from previous supervisor runs. They do not satisfy any gate by themselves.
1. [FM-1.1] Disobey task specification (source_run_id=2a2338da-3ef5-4a6e-a794-7d0ce6006b51): Repair the planning artifact or traceability gap before invoking the lead again.
2. [FM-1.1] Disobey task specification (source_run_id=40f4ecea-e8bd-4639-aec6-27d686743e8f): Repair the planning artifact or traceability gap before invoking the lead again.
3. [FM-1.5] Unaware of termination conditions (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Verify this known failure mode explicitly before claiming the gate is complete.
4. [FM-2.4] Information withholding (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Address independent reviewer objections with concrete evidence references.
5. [FM-1.3] Step repetition (source_run_id=f89bc699-1b4e-4713-95b7-d1493a3acfd8): Change the plan or evidence before retrying; do not repeat the same handoff.

claims: Generator clusters failure-taxonomy, reviewer-disagreement, probe-cohort-flip, and lesson signals by (task_class,gate,taxonomy_code) and drafts exactly one experiment per cluster at/over recurrence_threshold (generator.py:90-144; test :94).
Drafts cannot execute until operator activation; pre-activation runner returns [] and no workflow job is created (test :191-192).
Immutable-surface clusters become report_only with empty mutable_paths and cannot be promoted to runnable (test :272-304).
Weekly cap limits runnable starts per window via count_autoresearch_experiments_started_since (generator.py:235-238; test :307-352).
Rejected evaluator reports park the row as failed with explicit error (test :219-269).
signal_key UNIQUE enforces re-run dedup in all three schema lanes.

criteria: Intent realized: operator-gated draft->runnable->completed/failed lifecycle present (generator.py drafts:67, activate:183, run_runnable:222).
Durable lane (anti-bypass): run_autoresearch_fixture(execution_mode='live') :264-271 NOT subprocess; test :213 asserts dual_agent_workflow_jobs 0->1 only after activation.
Dedup idempotency: signal_key UNIQUE in SQLite (schema_migrations 0003 + inline), Postgres inline (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), Alembic 20260610_0003 down_revision 0002.
Safety invariants hardcoded False: default_change_allowed False (test :212), report_only stays report_only + runner==[] (test :272-304), gate not advanced.
Tests non-vacuous and map P1-P4 (config:56, repeated-taxonomy:94, reviewer/probe/lesson:126, draft-cannot-run:174, rejected->failed:266, immutable:272, weekly-cap:307).
File ownership exact: 11 changed files <-> git diff, no code orphans (other untracked = docs/.scratch), +640/-5 --check clean.
confidence.rationale: Intent fully realized and verified from source plus 7 non-vacuous tests referencing real symbols; durable lane and dedup invariants confirmed by reading code and all 3 schema lanes; diff scope exact (+640/-5, --check clean). Confidence capped below 0.9 because pytest/poll/shasum were permission-denied, so test_status is unknown and the specific durable job run is unconfirmed (acceptable per handoff self_reported grade).

payload.content: AutoResearch hypothesis-generation outcome ACCEPTED. Generator drafts operator-gated experiments from recurring failure signals and runs them via the durable evaluator lane. Intent fully realized across 4 slices; 7 non-vacuous tests map P1-P4; signal_key UNIQUE dedup enforced in all 3 schema lanes; all safety invariants hardcoded False. Residual: live durable run of workflow-1bdbea5238c1 and pytest unconfirmed (permission-denied), evidence grade self_reported per handoff.

assumptions_to_verify: That pytest would pass green if executed (tests are present, non-vacuous, and reference real symbols, but unexecuted).
That the on-disk artifact bytes equal the handoff sha256 values (content read-verified, hashes not recomputed).
That workflow-1bdbea5238c1 completed without error in the live ledger.
contradictions_checked: Intent says 'executed via the durable evaluator lane' vs implementation: confirmed run_autoresearch_fixture(execution_mode='live') :264-271 is the durable lane, NOT a subprocess shortcut, and test :213 proves a workflow job materializes only post-activation (no contradiction).

Intent says 'automatically from recurring signals' vs generator having only test callers + __init__ exports: issues.md Slice 2 scopes this as a library generator (no production scheduler required); 'automatic' = algorithmic drafting, not manual hypothesis authoring (no contradiction).
'Operator-activated' vs safety: report_only rows cannot be activated to runnable and runner skips them (test :272-304) — consistent.
Dedup claim vs schema: signal_key UNIQUE present in SQLite, Postgres inline, and Alembic 0003 — consistent across lanes.
missing_evidence: Executed pytest result (green/red) for the 7 generator tests + 2 migration-parity tests.
Live poll output for workflow-1bdbea5238c1 (status/recovery_point/pid/error).
shasum -a 256 confirmation that on-disk artifacts match handoff planning_artifacts hashes.
critical_review.strongest_objection: The live durable run of workflow-1bdbea5238c1 cannot be confirmed because the poll command, pytest, and shasum were all permission-denied; the gate therefore relies on source reading plus self_reported evidence rather than an executed end-to-end durable run.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: A failing/red pytest run on the generator or migration-parity tests; a poll showing workflow-1bdbea5238c1 errored or diverged from the queue terminal status; or signal_key UNIQUE missing from any one of the three schema lanes (which would break P1 dedup).
